request.summary: Integrate Change Observatory into the Agentic SDLC plugin and make it launch-ready after installation.

input_summaries: Approved Change Observatory requirement, integration contract v3, and completed core/UI evidence.
Existing portable plugin, CLI, package allowlist, output registry, and trace schemas.
output_summaries: Installed observe launcher, plugin-local launch skill, bundled UI/runtime, trace narratives, security hardening, and regression fixes.
narrative.rationale_summary: A dependency-free local server, installed-relative asset resolution, and a plugin-local skill preserve portability without global PATH or postinstall mutation.
alternatives: Hosted dashboard was rejected because it expands privacy, identity, deployment, and operating-cost boundaries.
Global PATH or postinstall shims were rejected because Codex plugin installation must remain portable and reversible.

explanation.text: Change Observatory now ships inside Agentic SDLC and can be opened from the installed plugin while reconstructing only recorded request, change, decision, contract, and verification evidence.

summary: Validated installed Change Observatory across automated, package, security, and real-browser paths

request.summary: Make the integrated Change Observatory functional and launch-ready after plugin installation.

input_summaries: Final plugin sources, npm allowlist, installed tarball, personal marketplace staging, and canonical real-project SDLC evidence.
output_summaries: All 165 automated tests passed with zero failures; package, doctor, plugin/skill validators, installed smoke, desktop, and 360px browser QA passed.
narrative.rationale_summary: Verification was layered so source-level success could not hide missing installed assets, unsafe local API behavior, or unusable responsive presentation.
alternatives: Source-checkout-only testing was rejected because it cannot prove the installed plugin contains and launches the app.

explanation.text: The app was tested from the packaged installation boundary and in a real browser, including authentication, path safety, semantic summaries, lineage, raw evidence, mobile navigation, and clean shutdown.
